name: dumbbell decline twist fly
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face forward at the bottom of the movement.
Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position, squeezing your chest muscles at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight up over your chest. Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest. As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face forward at the bottom of the movement. Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position, squeezing your chest muscles at the top. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
